title: sorghum schinzii
intro: Sorghum schinzii, also known as African finger grass, is a type of perennial grass belonging to the sorghum family. Traditionally, it has been used in African medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, among others. In herbal preparations, sorghum schinzii is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being applied topically in the form of ointments or creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
